# Feature: Response Download Fix — Consistent Download in VS Code & Web

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-008
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Make "Download response" actually produce a correct file with the right name and content type on both platforms. Today `ResponseBody.handleDownload` ([ResponseBody.tsx:107-114](../../packages/core/src/components/common/ResponseBody.tsx)) passes an **object** `{ body, fileName, contentType }` into a callback both apps type as `(data: string)` — vscode encodes `"[object Object]"` and hardcodes `response.json` ([vscode App.tsx:542-550](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx)); web calls `file.writeFile('response.json', data)` with the object — no visible effect ([web App.tsx:561-564](../../packages/web/src/App.tsx)).

**Additional verified findings (beyond the plan)**:
- **vscode message-shape mismatch**: `vsCodeAdapter.downloadResponse` posts `{ type, data: <base64 string>, filename, contentType }` ([vsCodeAdapter.ts:869-879](../../packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts)) but `MessageHandler.handleDownloadResponse` destructures `const { body, fileName } = message.data` ([MessageHandler.ts:842-848](../../packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts)) — even a string payload would fail. Both sides must be aligned.
- **DEVIATION**: the plan proposes a new web `downloadFile` adapter method, but `webAdapter.downloadResponse` ([webAdapter.ts:1128-1134](../../packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts)) **already implements** the Blob + object-URL + anchor download. The web fix is simply calling the existing `file.downloadResponse` instead of `writeFile`. No new adapter method needed.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persisted data. New core payload contract:

### Entities & Attributes

- **`ResponseDownloadPayload`** (new interface in `packages/core/src/types/` — e.g., alongside `ResponseData` in `collection.ts`):
  - `body` (string) — the response body **as held by `ResponseBody`** (base64-encoded transport form; `formatBody` decodes it for display, so the payload carries the original encoded string for byte-exact downloads of text *and* binary)
  - `fileName` (string) — generated by `ResponseBody` (timestamped, extension from content-type — already implemented)
  - `contentType` (string) — from response headers, default `application/octet-stream`
- **Callback type**: `onDownloadResponse(payload: ResponseDownloadPayload): void` threaded `ResponseBody` → `RequestEditor` (both platforms) → `App.tsx`.

### Relationships

- vscode chain: payload → App handler (base64 → `Uint8Array`) → `file.downloadResponse(bytes, fileName, contentType)` → postMessage → `MessageHandler.handleDownloadResponse` → VS Code save dialog.
- web chain: payload → App handler (base64 → `Uint8Array`) → `file.downloadResponse(bytes, fileName, contentType)` → Blob + anchor click.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Implementer must verify against `ResponseData.isEncoded` how `body` arrives (the display path always base64-decodes, indicating base64 transport); if any path delivers plain text, normalize **inside `handleDownload`** so the payload `body` is always base64 — one rule, both platforms.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/types/collection.ts` — `ResponseDownloadPayload` interface
- `packages/core/src/components/common/ResponseBody.tsx` — typed prop (`any` → payload), malformed-comment cleanup (~line 112 per plan; verify)
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx`, `packages/web/src/RequestEditor.tsx` (web equivalent) — typed prop pass-through
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/App.tsx` — handler uses payload (decode base64, real fileName/contentType)
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts` + `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts` — align the `downloadResponse` message shape
- `packages/web/src/App.tsx` — handler calls `file.downloadResponse` with payload data
- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/ResponseBody.test.tsx` — payload contract tests
- `packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts` — message shape test
- `docs/features/requests.md` — note the download behavior (filename from content-type)

## 4. Dependencies

- None.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: `ResponseDownloadPayload` type threaded through core

- [x] **Implementation**: Define `ResponseDownloadPayload` in core. Change `ResponseBodyProps.onDownloadResponse` from `(data: any)` to `(payload: ResponseDownloadPayload)`; ensure `handleDownload` always produces base64 `body` (normalize if a plain-text path exists per §2). Thread the type through both platform `RequestEditor.tsx` prop interfaces (replacing `(data: string)`). Clean up the malformed comment the plan flags near [ResponseBody.tsx:112](../../packages/core/src/components/common/ResponseBody.tsx) if present.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: **Regression-first** — `ResponseBody` test asserting the download button invokes the callback with `{ body: <base64>, fileName: matching /response_.*\.json/, contentType: 'application/json' }` for a JSON response (this pins the contract the old `(data: string)` handlers violated); binary response (e.g., `image/png`) produces `.png` filename and untouched base64 body.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the interface and the prop.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- ResponseBody` green; `pnpm typecheck` at root fails until both App handlers are updated (drives TASK-002/003), then passes.

### TASK-002: vscode download path — handler + message-shape alignment

- [x] **Implementation**:
  - `App.tsx` `handleDownloadResponse(payload)`: decode `payload.body` base64 → `Uint8Array` (shared `base64ToUint8Array`-style util; do **not** `TextEncoder.encode` the base64 text — decode it, byte-exact for text and binary alike) → `await file.downloadResponse(bytes, payload.fileName, payload.contentType)`; error → notification.
  - Align the message contract: `vsCodeAdapter.downloadResponse` sends `{ type: 'downloadResponse', data: { body: base64, fileName, contentType } }` and `MessageHandler.handleDownloadResponse` destructures exactly that (it already expects `data.body`/`data.fileName`); pass `contentType` into the save dialog's `filters` where sensible. Convert the adapter to `sendAndWait` so dialog-cancel/write errors surface as `Result` (it currently returns optimistic `ok` — same anti-pattern FEAT-003 removes for `saveCollection`).
- [x] **Unit Tests**: `MessageHandler.test.ts` — `downloadResponse` message with `{ body, fileName }` triggers save-dialog flow with the decoded bytes and suggested fileName; adapter test asserts the sent message shape matches what the handler reads (the regression that was silently broken).
- [x] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the adapter method documenting the wire shape.
- **Verify**: In the extension: download a JSON response → save dialog suggests `response_<timestamp>.json`, saved file content equals the response body; repeat with an image/binary response → bytes identical to the response.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: web download path — use the existing `downloadResponse` adapter

- [x] **Implementation**: Replace the `file.writeFile('response.json', data)` call in web `App.tsx` with: decode `payload.body` base64 → `Uint8Array` → `await file.downloadResponse(bytes, payload.fileName, payload.contentType)` (already implemented as Blob + object URL + anchor click — verify it revokes the object URL after click; add `URL.revokeObjectURL` if missing). Handle the `Result` error branch with a notification. *(DEVIATION from plan noted in §1: no new `downloadFile` adapter method is needed.)*
- [x] **Unit Tests**: webAdapter test — `downloadResponse` creates a Blob with the given type and triggers an anchor click with `download=fileName` (jsdom-level assertions); App-handler test — payload flows to the adapter decoded.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None beyond JSDoc.
- **Verify**: In the web app: download JSON and binary responses — browser downloads files with correct names, types, and byte-exact content; parity check with Copy (text content identical).
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: Both handlers check the adapter `Result` and surface errors via `notification.showNotification('error', …)`; the vscode adapter conversion to `sendAndWait` removes another optimistic-`ok` mutation (plan architecture rule).
- **Logging**: Log download failures with fileName/contentType context only — **never** the body (may contain sensitive response data).
